snappySummary: An absolute circus. Almiron got sent off within forty minutes for swearing at the ref. Paraguay held on for a chaotic 1-0 win.
talkingPoints: Galarza scores the opener in 64 seconds;Almiron's red card completely derailed Türkiye's defensive blueprint;Paraguay's physical display ran the clock down effectively
randomQuirk: Almiron covering his mouth to swear at the referee, only to get caught by high-definition cameras.

snappySummary: Morocco danced through the Scottish midfield and claimed a deserved 2-1 victory. Scotland's late surge was too little, too late.
talkingPoints: Morocco's liquid football carved open the Scottish double-decker bus;Conceded to an overhead kick that defied all laws of Scottish gravity;Morocco's defense stood tall under late aerial pressure
randomQuirk: A Scottish fan in a kilt spotted reading a tactical guidebook upside-down in the 88th minute.

snappySummary: The USA cruised to a 3-0 victory over Australia. The Socceroos looked like they were having a casual kickabout in the park.
talkingPoints: Pulisic was a walking cheat code, scoring two and assisting one;Australia completely bottled it in possession, committing 18 turnovers;The high press from the Americans left Australia gasping for air
randomQuirk: An Australian defender trying to clear the ball, only to kick it directly into his own teammate's face.

snappySummary: Brazil dominated Haiti 5-0 in a match that felt like a training session. Vinicius Jr scored a hat-trick of pure class.
talkingPoints: Vinicius Jr put on a show with three goals and endless flair;Haiti's Sunday League defending conceded three goals from simple corners;Brazil hit the post three times, preventing an even bigger scoreline
randomQuirk: A Haitian defender asking Vinicius Jr to swap shirts during a water break in the 30th minute.

snappySummary: The Netherlands put on an absolute clinic, tearing Sweden to shreds with five goals, leaving the Swedes looking like they'd forgotten how to defend. A proper thumping!
talkingPoints: Brian Brobbey and Cody Gakpo both bagging a brace? Talk about liquid football! They were absolutely unplayable.;Denzel Dumfries, with two assists, was flying down that wing like a man possessed. What a performance!;Sweden's defending was more 'Sunday League' than 'World Cup'. Three yellow cards and conceding five? Woeful.;Despite the scoreline, Sweden actually had more shots and shots on target. Just goes to show, it's about putting them away, isn't it?
randomQuirk: Sweden managed to rack up three yellow cards, proving that if you can't beat 'em, at least try to kick 'em. Classy.

editionTitle: Germany's Great Escape: Undav to the Rescue!
snappySummary: Germany snatched a dramatic late victory against a spirited Côte d'Ivoire, with Deniz Undav proving to be the hero, bagging a brace including a last-gasp winner. Squeaky bum time for the Germans!
talkingPoints: Franck Kessie's opener for Côte d'Ivoire had Germany rattled, but they showed real character to come back.;Deniz Undav, coming off the bench, turned into a walking cheat code, scoring both goals to save Germany's blushes.;Côte d'Ivoire's keeper, Yahia Fofana, was a busy man, making five saves and keeping them in it for so long.;Germany's 59.5% possession and 16 shots show they dominated, but it took them until the dying embers to secure the win.
randomQuirk: Germany had three substitutions at the 60-minute mark, a clear sign the gaffer wasn't happy and threw the kitchen sink at it. It paid off, didn't it?

editionTitle: The Goalless Snoozefest: Ecuador and Curaçao Share the Spoils (and the Blame)
snappySummary: A truly forgettable affair saw Ecuador and Curaçao play out a drab 0-0 draw. Ecuador had all the ball and all the shots, but couldn't hit a barn door, while Curaçao's keeper was an absolute hero.
talkingPoints: Ecuador had a staggering 74.6% possession and 27 shots, with 15 on target, yet somehow failed to score. You'd think they were having a casual kickabout in the park!;Eloy Room, Curaçao's goalkeeper, put in an absolute shift, making a ridiculous 15 saves. He was a brick wall!;Curaçao picked up five yellow cards, clearly frustrated by Ecuador's inability to finish, or perhaps just by the sheer boredom of it all.;Both teams managed to avoid scoring despite a combined 37 shots. Clinical finishing? Never heard of it, mate.
randomQuirk: Curaçao's five yellow cards were almost as numerous as their shots on target (3). At least they were trying to make an impression, eh?

editionTitle: Tunisia vs Japan: The Blue Samurai's Masterclass
snappySummary: Japan absolutely ran riot, putting four past a hapless Tunisia who looked like they were having a casual kickabout in the park.
talkingPoints: Japan's clinical finishing was a joy to behold, especially Ayase Ueda bagging a brace and an assist.;Tunisia's defence was more open than a motorway service station, conceding four goals and managing zero shots on target.;The drinks breaks seemed to be the only thing slowing Japan down as they dominated with 62.1% possession.
randomQuirk: Tunisia managed a grand total of 0 shots on target despite having 37.9% possession, proving that sometimes, just sometimes, football isn't coming home for everyone.

editionTitle: Spain's Stroll in the Park Against Saudi Arabia
snappySummary: Spain put on a clinic, effortlessly dispatching Saudi Arabia with four goals, including a rather unfortunate own goal.
talkingPoints: Mikel Oyarzabal was a menace, grabbing two goals and an assist before half-time.;Lamine Yamal showed why he's the real deal, opening the scoring early on with one of his five shots.;Saudi Arabia's defence had a tough day at the office, topped off with an own goal – classic.;Spain's 67.1% possession meant Saudi Arabia were chasing shadows for most of the match, managing only 3 shots.
randomQuirk: Saudi Arabia's Hassan Al Tambakti decided to get in on the scoring action, unfortunately for his own side, with a delightful own goal.

editionTitle: Belgium and Iran Play Out a Thrilling... 0-0 Draw
snappySummary: A truly forgettable affair saw Belgium and Iran cancel each other out in a goalless draw, with Belgium even managing a red card for good measure.
talkingPoints: Belgium had 23 shots but couldn't hit a barn door with a banjo, managing a paltry 0 goals despite 70.1% possession.;Nathan Ngoy's red card for Belgium added a bit of spice to an otherwise bland encounter.;Iran's keeper, Alireza Beiranvand, was the busiest man on the pitch, making 7 saves.;Romelu Lukaku picked up an early yellow card, clearly more interested in a wrestling match than scoring.
randomQuirk: Belgium, with 70.1% possession and 23 shots, still couldn't find the back of the net, proving possession truly is nine-tenths of the law, but goals win games.

editionTitle: Uruguay and Cape Verde Share the Spoils in a Four-Goal Thriller
snappySummary: Uruguay and Cape Verde played out a pulsating 2-2 draw, with both sides showing flashes of brilliance and moments of Sunday League defending.
talkingPoints: Cape Verde took an early lead from a free-kick, stunning Uruguay with Kevin Pina's strike.;Uruguay fought back with two quick goals before half-time, only to concede again in the second half.;Maxi Araújo was instrumental for Uruguay, scoring one and assisting another.;Cape Verde's Hélio Varela came off the bench and scored a cracker from outside the box.
randomQuirk: Uruguay had 11 corners to Cape Verde's 4, but still couldn't find a winner, showing that sometimes quantity doesn't quite beat quality.

editionTitle: Egypt Come From Behind to See Off New Zealand
snappySummary: New Zealand gave Egypt a scare by taking the lead, but the Pharaohs rallied in the second half to secure a comfortable 3-1 victory.
talkingPoints: New Zealand's Finn Surman gave them a shock lead with a header from a corner, assisted by Tim Payne.;Mohamed Salah was a walking cheat code, scoring and assisting for Egypt, demonstrating his class with 5 shots.;Mostafa Zico and Trezeguet also got on the scoresheet for Egypt, showing their attacking depth.;New Zealand's two yellow cards suggest they were a bit too eager to get stuck in, with Sarpreet Singh and Callum McCowatt both booked.
randomQuirk: New Zealand's Finn Surman scored with their first shot on target, giving them a fleeting moment of glory before Egypt decided to wake up.

snappySummary: Messi missed a penalty, then decided to score two anyway, because, well, he's Messi. Argentina cruised past Austria, who are now officially booking their flights home.
talkingPoints: Lionel Messi, the cheeky blighter, missed a penalty early doors but still managed to bag a brace, proving he's still a walking cheat code.;Argentina dominated possession with 53.6% and had 12 shots, showing they were well in control, despite a few injury stoppages.;Austria managed a paltry 1 shot on target from 6 attempts, which isn't exactly going to win you any World Cups, is it?;Plenty of stoppages for injuries and drinks breaks, making it feel a bit like a casual kickabout in the park rather than a high-stakes World Cup tie.
randomQuirk: Messi missed a penalty in the 9th minute, then decided to score two goals later, just to show he can do whatever he fancies.

editionTitle: France vs. Iraq: Mbappé's Stroll in the Park
snappySummary: France, led by a rampant Mbappé, made light work of Iraq, who barely laid a glove on them. A comfortable win for the French, who are now safely through.
talkingPoints: Kylian Mbappé was having a casual kickabout, bagging two goals and proving once again why he's one of the best.;France registered a whopping 19 shots with 5 on target, showing their attacking prowess, while Iraq managed zero shots on target from 4 attempts – not exactly a threat, were they?;Michael Olise grabbed two assists, pulling the strings nicely for the French attack.;Iraq's Amir Al Ammari picked up a yellow card for a bad foul, perhaps out of sheer frustration at being run ragged.
randomQuirk: Iraq's goalkeeper, Ahmed Basil, made 2 saves, but his outfield players couldn't muster a single shot on target, making his job a bit like trying to stop a tide with a colander.

editionTitle: Norway vs. Senegal: Haaland Delivers, Senegal Head Home
snappySummary: Erling Haaland scored another two goals, because of course he did, as Norway saw off Senegal in a surprisingly open game. Senegal's spirited effort wasn't enough, and they're now out.
talkingPoints: Erling Haaland, the walking cheat code, bagged another brace, proving he's absolutely lethal in front of goal.;Senegal showed some fight with Ismaïla Sarr scoring two goals, but their defence looked a bit like Sunday League defending at times.;Norway had less possession (41.7%) but were more clinical, with 7 shots on target from 13 attempts.;Senegal's goalkeeper Édouard Mendy had to be substituted due to injury, adding insult to injury for the eliminated side.
randomQuirk: Senegal had more possession (58.3%) and more shots (16) than Norway, yet still managed to lose, proving that sometimes, you just can't buy a win.

editionTitle: Jordan vs. Algeria: Late Drama Sees Algeria Through
snappySummary: Algeria left it late, coming from behind to snatch a win against Jordan, who had a sniff but ultimately bottled it. Algeria are through, Jordan are packing their bags.
talkingPoints: Jordan took a surprising lead through Nizar Al Rashdan, giving their fans a moment of hope before Algeria turned the screw.;Algeria dominated possession with a whopping 72.1% and had 17 shots, showing their intent, even if it took them a while to convert.;Nadhir Benbouali and Amine Gouiri scored the crucial goals for Algeria, both coming from set-pieces or close-range finishes, showing a bit of grit.;Jordan's goalkeeper Yazeed Abulaila made 6 saves, trying his best to keep his side in it against the Algerian onslaught.
randomQuirk: Jordan managed to score with just 27.9% possession, proving that sometimes you just need to be clinical, even if it doesn't always last.
